data.body:
Scope confirmed (Michael, 2026-07-08): proceed on the CURRENT rules-engine architecture. (a) Collapse multi-match to the single highest-priority exemption instead of returning an unsorted list; (b) drop age (under-19/over-65) from the exclusion path per Jeff — state won't send out-of-age-range members (see also CMS §435.553: age is a mandatory exception, not exclusion); (c) remove now-dead DOB/evaluated_on extraction.

FORWARD NOTES for the future ARIES API adapter (out of scope here):
1. Name reconciliation — the ARIES contract (Data Dictionary spec, doc 1cBEz…) sends a flat `member_data.exclusions_and_exceptions[]` array of `{name, value, verification_status}` with a 4th naming vocabulary that diverges from our config ids: `american_indian_or_alaska_native` (the "or" returns), `pregnant_or_postpartum`, `medical_condition` (≈ medically_frail), `caregiver_child`/`caregiver_disability`, `complies_with_atap/snap_work_requirements`, `substance_treatment`, `inmate`+`recently_released` (≈ our `incarceration`). Adapter must map explicitly — do NOT string-munge (extends the id↔fact drift risk already noted below).
2. `verification_status` (verified/unverified) becomes a factor the selection logic must weigh — not modeled today.
3. Compute-vs-receive + dates is UNRESOLVED in the spec: the request shape sends pre-computed booleans (no dates → OSCER just picks), but Jeff D (spec comment 2026-07-07) argues OSCER should make time-based judgments (exclusion true during lookback but not now → "excepted"; 12-mo postpartum window), which needs per-exemption dates. If that lands, "stop-at-first == best" must be revisited (the PM dynamic-durability flag) and dates reintroduced in a new shape.

The return-best SELECTION logic built here is durable across the array-model rewrite; only the input plumbing gets replaced.
